title: Online tyre-cliff changepoint features from residualized stint sequences
summary: Recent F1 tyre research models degradation as a latent state with pit-stop resets.
rationale: Recent F1 tyre research models degradation as a latent state with pit-stop resets. Separately, changepoint methods are built to detect short-series regime shifts. That suggests a more specific feature family than generic rolling means: detect the first local break where pace/degradation moves from smooth wear to cliff behavior.
implementation_hint: Within each `(Year,Race,Driver,Stint)`, residualize lap-time delta and degradation against lap-level field/compound means, then compute online changepoint proxies: cumulative sum shocks, breakpoint probability, last-break distance, pre/post-break slope, and cliff intensity. Use only past laps when constructing them.
expected_effect: Should help separate normal old-tyre laps from imminent-stop laps where degradation suddenly accelerates.
risk: Short stints and noisy wet laps can create false breaks; needs compound- and weather-aware smoothing.
